name: golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev
title: golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev
summary: Go library for detecting and expanding the user's home directory
description:
Go library for detecting and expanding the user's home directory
This is a Go library for detecting the user's home directory without the use of
cgo, so the library can be used in cross-compilation environments.
.
Usage is incredibly simple, just call homedir.Dir() to get the home directory
for a user, and homedir.Expand() to expand the ~ in a path to the home
directory.
.
Why not just use os/user? The built-in os/user package requires cgo on Darwin
systems. This means that any Go code that uses that package cannot cross
compile. But 99% of the time the use for os/user is just to retrieve the home
directory, which we can do for the current user without cgo. This library does
that, enabling cross-compilation.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev because Go library for detecting and expanding the user's home directory.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-mitchellh-go-homedir-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
